summary: Frontend-only testing of shared CustomerSignupFields component used on both /signup page and Admin Create Customer dialog. All 6 test scenarios verified: fields present in correct schema order, labels from schema, no cursor loss on typing, and customer creation succeeds.
test_credentials: Partner code: automate-accounts | Admin: admin@automateaccounts.local / ChangeMe123! | localStorage: aa_partner_code='automate-accounts' required for /signup
seed_data_creation: Created test customer 'Test Customer 001' (testcustomer001@test.example.com) in admin panel during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: CustomerSignupFields shared component is working correctly on both /signup page and Admin Create Customer dialog. Fields are in correct schema order (Full Name → Email → Password → Company Name → Job Title → Phone → Address), labels come from schema (not hardcoded), cursor retention works for all input fields (no focus loss), and customer creation via admin dialog succeeds. The /signup page requires localStorage key 'aa_partner_code'='automate-accounts' to be set BEFORE navigating to /signup (navigate to root first, set localStorage, then navigate to /signup).

evidence: Full Name field: data-testid='signup-field-full_name' - present and visible
Email field: data-testid='signup-email-input' - present and visible
Password field: data-testid='signup-password-input' - present and visible
Company Name field: data-testid='signup-field-company_name' - present and visible
Job Title field: data-testid='signup-field-job_title' - present and visible
Phone field: data-testid='signup-field-phone' - present and visible
Address section with Line 1, Line 2, City, State/Province, Postal, Country - all present
Field order: Full Name(y=267) → Email(y=267) → Password(y=335) → Company Name(y=335) → Job Title(y=440) → Phone(y=440) - correct schema order
Labels from schema (not hardcoded): FULL NAME, EMAIL ADDRESS, PASSWORD, COMPANY NAME, JOB TITLE, PHONE
Full Name: typed 'John Doe', got 'John Doe' - NO cursor loss
Email: typed 'test@example.com', got 'test@example.com' - NO cursor loss
Password: typed 'TestPass1!', got 'TestPass1!' - NO cursor loss
All 6 fields present: Full Name, Email, Password, Company Name, Job Title, Phone
Address section present: Line 1*, Line 2 (optional), City*, State/Province, Postal*, Country*
Field order: Full Name(y=224) → Email(y=284) → Password(y=344) → Company Name(y=441) → Job Title(y=501) → Phone(y=561) - correct
Full Name: typed 'Test User Create', got 'Test User Create' - NO cursor loss
Email: typed 'testcreate@example.com', got 'testcreate@example.com' - NO cursor loss
Password: typed 'TestPass1!Secure', got 'TestPass1!Secure' - NO cursor loss
Filled: Full Name='Test Customer 001', Email='testcustomer001@test.example.com', Password='TestPass1!Secure', Address Line 1='123 Test Street', City='Toronto', Postal='M5V 3A8', Country='Canada'
Toast: 'Customer created'
Dialog closed after creation
New customer 'Test Customer 001' appeared in customers table with Canada as country
